questions.title: What is the name of the operation under which Pakistan has carried out precision strikes to response Indian aggression in May 2025?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan launched precision missile strikes against India under Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos.

Bunyan Ul Marsoos is an Arabic verse from the Quran.

The operation’s name—meaning “a structure firmly joined together”—draws inspiration from Surah Al-Saff (61-4), which praises those who fight in Allah’s cause as a solid, fortified structure.

إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَانٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ

Translation: ‘Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead’ (Surah Al-Saff, verse 4).

This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

Seven sites in India were targeted, including the key Pathankot air base, Udhampur air base, and Gujarat air base. Rajasthan air base and Brahmos storage site.

The operation was launched in retaliation for Indian missile strikes that hit several locations inside Pakistan.

 ****

Mir Jafar Khan Jamali, a prominent Baloch leader, played a pivotal role in mobilizing support for the Muslim League in Balochistan during the Pakistan Movement.

He worked closely with Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah and helped convince the people of Balochistan to join Pakistan.

questions.title: When did Fatima Jinnah become the head of the women’s wing of the All India Muslim League?
questions.explanation:
Fatima Jinnah became the head of the women's wing of the All India Muslim League in 1938.

She played a significant role in mobilizing women for the cause of Pakistan and the political activities of the Muslim League.

questions.title: Who among the following served as an Air Commodore in Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
Cecil Chaudhry was a distinguished Pakistani fighter pilot.

He served as an Air Commodore in the Pakistan Air Force.

He was also a war hero in the 1965 and 1971 wars.

He was also later became an educationist and human rights activist.

questions.title: When did the Shahi Jirga of Baluchistan decide to join Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
The province's shahi jirga (royal council) decided to join Pakistan on 29 June 1947.

The princely states except Kalat acceded to Pakistan.

questions.title: What are the boundaries of a map called?
questions.explanation:
The margin refers to the boundary or edge of a map, framing the content within.

It usually contains important map information like title, scale, and legend placement.

questions.title: Begum Jahan Ara Shah Nawaz wrote several pamphlets on which of the following topics?
questions.explanation:
Begum Jahan Ara Shah Nawaz focused on the rights and upliftment of women in her writings.

questions.title: Who established the Muslim League in Balochistan on the instructions of Quaid-e-Azam in 1939?
questions.explanation:
Qazi Muhammad Isa established the Muslim League in Balochistan in 1939 on the instructions of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

He served as the President of All-India Muslim League's Balochistan branch.

questions.title: According to 1941 Census ______ of the population of Kashmir state was Muslim.
questions.explanation:
In the last census of British India in 1941, the total population of Kashmir and Jammu was 3,945,000.

questions.title: According to UNO report Pakistan is at _____ position in the list of countries facing water crises.
questions.explanation:
According to some sources, Pakistan is in the top 10 countries facing water scarcity, and specifically, the 7th position according to the UNO report.

Other reports place Pakistan at 3rd or 14th among countries with high water risk.

questions.title: After the reading lesson women's role in the Pakistan movement. It is inferred that the role of women expended because of _____?
questions.answer: Quaid-e-Azam who stressed the importance of women as equal partner of men in the struggle for a separate homeland
questions.explanation:
Quaid-e-Azam emphasized that women were equal partners in the movement for Pakistan.

His encouragement led to a broader and more active role of women in the independence struggle.

questions.explanation:

questions.title: Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in ___?
questions.explanation:
From 1947 to 1973, Pakistan had a unicameral legislature with only the National Assembly.

The 1973 Constitution introduced a bicameral system, adding the Senate alongside the National Assembly.

questions.title: Which is Pakistan 2nd largest foreign exchange earner crop?
questions.explanation:
• Rice is Pakistan's second-largest foreign exchange earner crop after cotton.
  • Pakistan ranks as the 11th largest rice producer globally.
  • It is the 5th largest rice exporter in the world.

questions.title: Artificial Forests being maintained in ______?
questions.explanation:
• Changa Manga is one of the largest man-made forests in Pakistan, located in Punjab.
  • It was originally planted during the British colonial era to provide timber for railway networks.

questions.title: Chandka Medical college is located at ______?
questions.explanation:
Chandka is the old name of Larkana.

Larakana is also called city of Guavas.

Larkana is a city located in Sindh.

questions.explanation:

questions.title: The biggest market of Pakistan export and import is ______?

Additional information

questions.title: Which division "Swat" District belongs to?
questions.explanation:

questions.title: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk prepared ______?
questions.explanation:
Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk played a key role in drafting the demands of the Simla Deputation (1906).

The delegation, led by Aga Khan III, met Lord Minto (the Viceroy) and presented demands for separate electorates and safeguards for Muslim political rights.

questions.title: The most important and famous Urdu newspaper published from Lahore by Maulana Zafar Ali khan?
questions.explanation:
The founder of ‘Zamindar’ (newspaper) was Maulana Zafar Ali Khan.
He is generally considered to be "the father of Urdu journalism."

questions.title: The Silver Fibre of Pakistan is ________?
questions.explanation:
Cotton is known as the "Silver Fibre" of Pakistan due to its economic importance and export value.

It plays a key role in Pakistan’s textile industry, the backbone of the country's economy.

questions.title: Who is known as the King of Qawwali?
questions.explanation:
Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan born October 1948.

King of Qawwali, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan died on August 16, 1997 of a sudden cardiac arrest in London at the age of 48.

questions.title: Who was the first Mughal Emperor to allow Britishers to establish trade links with India?
questions.explanation:
• Jahangir was the first Mughal Emperor to allow Britishers to establish trade links with India.
  • He gave permission for trade to the British East India Company, allowing them to establish a factory at Surat in 1613.
  • This marked the beginning of British trade in India during the Mughal era.

questions.title: According to Iqbal's philosophy it is necessary for a student ______?
questions.answer: Not look upon facts from others points of view
questions.explanation:
According to Iqbal’s philosophy, a student should think independently and not just accept others’ viewpoints blindly.

This emphasizes self-thinking and personal understanding rather than following others.

questions.title: In 1859 Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established/set up a first school where Persian and English were taught?

questions.title: During Alla-ud-Din period Mongols invaded Delhi at least ____ times between 1296 to 1306 AD. But each time they were repulsed.
questions.explanation:
During Alla-ud-Din Khilji's reign (1296-1306 AD), the Mongols invaded Delhi at least six times.

Despite their repeated invasions, Alla-ud-Din successfully repulsed them each time, demonstrating his military strength.

questions.title: Which was a time, when man began making tools and weapons from metals. This period was 2500 BC to 1500 BC?
questions.explanation:
The Bronze Age (2500 BC to 1500 BC) is when humans started making tools and weapons using bronze, an alloy of copper and tin.

It marked significant advancements in metallurgy and technology.

It leading to improvements in agriculture, warfare, and trade.

questions.title: The British established an organization "The East India Company" for trade purposes and work about how many years for the settlement?
questions.explanation:
The East India Company (EIC) was established in 1600 and effectively dissolved in 1858 after the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

Duration: ~258 years

Key Phases:

1600–1757: Trade monopoly (spices, textiles).

1757–1858: Political control (Battle of Plassey to Crown rule).

questions.title: What was the approximate population of Pakistan in the year 2010?

questions.title: The _____ were/was the founder of Vedic Culture?
questions.explanation:
The Aryans are considered the founders of Vedic culture, which emerged around 1500 BCE in India.

They brought the Vedic texts and formed the foundation of Hinduism and early Indian civilization.

questions.title: In the First Round Table, all the parties were represented except the _____?
In which movement the congress leader Mahatma Gandhi announced to support the Muslims completely?
questions.explanation:
Mahatma Gandhi supported the Khilafat Movement (1919–1924) to gain Muslim support against British rule.

It was a joint Hindu-Muslim effort to protest the dismemberment of the Ottoman Caliphate after World War I.

questions.title: When the recommendations of the Simon Commission were rejected by the Indians Public, a British Minister _____ passed insulting remarks that the Indian political parties were not able to present any proposals.
questions.explanation:
Lord Birkenhead, the British Secretary of State for India, insulted Indian leaders, claiming they couldn't agree on a constitution.

His remarks prompted Indian leaders to form the Nehru Report (1928) as a counter-proposal.

questions.title: Who is called the prisoner of Malta?
questions.explanation:
Maulana Muhammad-ul-Hassan (also known as Hasrat Mohani) was famously called the Prisoner of Malta due to his imprisonment by the British.

He was jailed for his anti-British activities and later exiled to Malta, hence the title.

questions.title: On which date Pakistan celebrated Youm-e-Tashakkur after giving India befitting response?
questions.explanation:
PM Shahbaz Sharif announced Youm-e-Tashakkur to celebrate success of 'Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos’ on 11th May 2025 wherein Pakistan gave befitting response to Indian aggression.

Youm-e-Tashakkur is called thanks giving day.


 Additional Information: 

This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

***

questions.title: General Elections for ______ legislature were held in 1945.
questions.explanation:
The 1945 general elections in British India were held for the Central Legislature (Central Legislative Assembly).

The General Election of 1945-46 was held in 11 provinces of British India.

It included the provinces under the British rule, excluding princely states.

Lord Wavell was the Viceroy of India at the time.

questions.title: The Cripps Mission proposed ______ for India.
questions.answer: Independence at the end of WW-II
questions.explanation:
The Cripps Mission (1942) proposed that India would be granted full independence after the Second World War.

It also offered the right to provinces to opt out, which was rejected by major Indian political parties.

questions.title: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, in the early phase of his politics, was a strong supporter of _______?
questions.explanation:
In the early phase of his political career, Sir Syed Ahmad Khan strongly advocated for Hindu-Muslim unity to ensure cooperation and peace under British rule.

He believed both communities could work together for the progress of India before later emphasizing Muslim identity separately.

questions.title: Pakistan and Iran were members of ______?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan and Iran were members of RCD (Regional Cooperation for Development) formed in 1964, and later ECO (Economic Cooperation Organization), which succeeded RCD in 1985.

Both are also members of D-8 (Developing-8), an organization for development cooperation among 8 Muslim-majority countries.

questions.title: The oldest known book of the Pashto language is considered to be ______?
questions.explanation:
Pata Khazana is considered the oldest known book in the Pashto language, claimed to date back to the 18th century.

It is a manuscript compiling biographies and poetry of earlier Pashto poets.

questions.title: Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was appointed as First Governor General of Pakistan on ______ August 1947.
questions.explanation:
Quaid e Azam worked as governor-general of Pakistan for 13 months

from 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

He was the 1st Governor-General of Pakistan.

Succeeded by: Khawaja Nazimuddin

He died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi

questions.title: Who wrote Kitab Tarikh-i-Awila in the 13th century?
questions.explanation:
Bayazid Ansari, a 13th-century scholar, wrote Kitab Tarikh-i-Awila.

It is a historical text focusing on the early history of the region.

questions.title: Who was the President of Pakistan, removed by General Pervez Musharraf in June 2001?
questions.explanation:
Rafique Tarar, the former President of Pakistan, resigned during Pervez Musharraf's presidency.


History

Muhammad Rafiq Tarar served as the ninth president of Pakistan

He was the 28th Chief Justice of Lahore High Court from 1989 to 1991

He  was born on November 1929 – died on 7 March 2022

questions.title: How many letters were exchanged during the Jinnah-Gandhi talks?
questions.explanation:
The Jinnah-Gandhi talks occurred from September 9 to September 27, 1944.

During this period, 24 letters were exchanged between Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.title: Maulvi Fazlul Haq was from the province of ______?
questions.explanation:
Maulvi Fazlul Haq was a prominent leader from Bengal.

He played a significant role in the political landscape of British India.

He is often referred to as the "Sher-e-Bengal" (Tiger of Bengal).

questions.title: What event in 1867 changed Sir Syed's viewpoint on Indian nationhood?
questions.explanation:
The Hindi-Urdu controversy of 1867 significantly changed Sir Syed Ahmed Khan's viewpoint on Indian nationhood.

This controversy highlighted the growing divide between Hindus and Muslims.

It influencing Sir Syed to focus on promoting education and social reforms among Muslims, leading to his support for a separate identity for Muslims in India.

questions.title: What significant change did the British make to Mughal coins under Akbar II?
questions.explanation:
Under Akbar II, the British removed Persian inscriptions from the Mughal coins.

This was part of their effort to reduce Mughal authority and establish British supremacy in India.

questions.title: Which empire had control over Gandhara from 486 BCE to 522 BCE?
questions.explanation:
The Achaemenid Persian Empire ruled over Gandhara from around 522 BCE to 486 BCE, during the reign of Darius I.

Gandhara became a satrapy (province) of the Persian Empire after being annexed under Darius’s expansion into the Indian subcontinent.

questions.title: Which government is responsible for removing encroachments from public roads and organizing cattle markets and local fairs?
questions.explanation:
The Local Government is responsible for managing municipal affairs, including removing encroachments from public roads and organizing local events such as cattle markets and fairs.

They handle administrative duties related to local development and public services within a specific region or locality.

questions.title: Which ministry was Sardar Nishtar appointed to in 1946?
questions.explanation:
Muslim League Ministers in the Interim Government of 1946:

  • Liaquat Ali Khan – Finance (Introduced the "Poor Man's Budget")
  • I. I. Chundrigar – Commerce
  • Abdur Rab Nishtar – Communications
  • Ghazanfar Ali Khan – Health
  • Jogendra Nath Mandal – Law (Legislature)

Liaquat Ali Khan's "Poor Man's Budget" focused on redistributing wealth and addressing the economic disparity between the rich and the poor.

questions.title: Pakistan has been elected as a non-permanent member of which UN body?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan has been elected as a non-permanent member of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) for the 2025–2026 term.

This marks the eighth time Pakistan has secured a seat on the 15-member council.

It is responsible for maintaining international peace and security.

questions.title: Who currently runs most of the courier services in different cities of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
Most courier services in Pakistan, such as TCS, Leopard, and M&P, are operated by private companies.

They offer faster and more flexible delivery services compared to government-run options.

questions.title: Which leader supported the Lahore Resolution (1940) from U.P?
questions.explanation:
The Lahore Resolution (1940), which laid the foundation for the creation of Pakistan, was supported by Chaudhary Khaliquzzaman, a prominent Muslim leader from United Provinces (U.P.) (now Uttar Pradesh).

His advocacy made him a key figure in the 1940s Pakistan Movement

More Details

Other leaders listed (Maulana Abdul Hamid, Aurangzeb Khan, I.I. Chundreegar) were not directly associated with the Lahore Resolution from U.P.

questions.title: What decision did Maharaja Hari Singh make that ignored the will of the people?
questions.answer: He decided to accede to India
questions.explanation:
Maharaja Hari Singh of Jammu and Kashmir decided to accede to India in 1947, ignoring the will of the people, many of whom favored joining Pakistan.

This decision led to significant political and military conflict between India and Pakistan over the Kashmir region.

questions.title: Which ancient civilization is associated with Tunic and Toga?
questions.explanation:
The tunic and toga were both distinctive garments worn in Ancient Rome.

The tunic was a basic garment, and the toga was a formal attire worn by Roman citizens, especially for public events.

questions.title: Which national day in Pakistan was initiated by Qazi Hussain Ahmad?
questions.explanation:
Kashmir Solidarity Day on 5th February was initiated by Qazi Hussain Ahmad in 1990 to express support for the Kashmiri people.

It became an official public holiday in Pakistan, observed annually to highlight the Kashmir issue.

questions.title: Who presented the first resolution in favor of an independent Muslim state in the Sindh Assembly?
questions.explanation:
The first resolution in favor of an independent Muslim state in the Sindh Assembly was presented by Sheikh Abdul Majeed Sindhi.

He proposed that the All India Muslim League create a constitutional scheme for Muslims to achieve full independence.

questions.title: Which of the following tributaries is not part of the Indus river system?
questions.explanation:
Porali River is not a part of the Indus River system; it flows in Balochistan and drains into the Arabian Sea separately.

Bolan, Chenab, and Panjkora are all tributaries of the Indus River system in Pakistan.

questions.title: “Musalman's are a nation according to any definition of nation and they must have their own homeland.” These are the words of ______?
questions.explanation:
Jinnah in his speech espoused the ‘two-nation theory’, saying: 

“Mussalmans are a nation according to any definition of nation, and they must have their own homeland, their territory, and their state...

We wish our people to develop to the fullest spiritual, cultural, economic, social and political life in a way that we think best and in consonance with our own ideals and according to the genius of our people”.

questions.title: What was one of the key reasons for the rift between Nawaz Sharif and General Musharraf before the 1999 coup?
questions.explanation:
One of the key reasons for the rift between Nawaz Sharif and General Pervez Musharraf before the 1999 coup was the Kargil conflict.

Nawaz Sharif was unhappy with the way the conflict was handled, particularly the lack of proper consultation with him by the military leadership, which led to tensions.

questions.title: Who initiated the Commission on National Education in Pakistan in 1959?
questions.explanation:
The Sharif Commission, also known as the National Commission on Education.

It was initiated by the government of President Muhammad Ayub Khan in 1959.

This commission was formed with the goal of reforming Pakistan's educational system to better meet the needs of the nation.

The commission was headed by S.M. Sharif.

questions.title: سید محمد راشد روضہ دھنی رحمۃ اللہ علیہ کہاں پیدا ہوئے؟
questions.explanation:

questions.title: What type of state was Pakistan envisioned as in the 1956 Constitution?
questions.explanation:
The 1956 Constitution declared Pakistan as an Islamic republic aiming to be a welfare state.

It emphasized social justice, equality, and the well-being of all citizens regardless of religion or background.

questions.title: Barrages have helped convert which type of land into fertile and productive areas?
questions.explanation:
Barrages control river water and divert it for irrigation.

This helps convert barren land into fertile and productive agricultural areas.

questions.title: Which empire ruled Gandhara from 522–486 BCE?
questions.explanation:
Gandhara was ruled by the Achaemenid Empire (Iranians) during 522–486 BCE, under Darius I.

They included Gandhara as a satrapy (province) in their vast empire.

questions.title: When the Pakistan Movement was at its peak, who ruled over Gilgit-Baltistan?
questions.explanation:
At the peak of the Pakistan Movement, Gilgit-Baltistan was under the control of the Maharaja of Kashmir, as it was a part of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir.

In 1947, following the partition, the region was later administered by Pakistan after the Kashmir conflict.

questions.title: What movement did Chaudhry Rahmat Ali start while in England?
questions.explanation:
Chaudhry Rahmat Ali started the Pakistan National Movement in England in 1933.

He is credited with coining the name "Pakistan" and advocating for a separate Muslim state.

questions.title: What was the name of Qazi Hussain Ahmad’s father?
questions.explanation:
Born: 12 January 1938 in Nowshera, NWFP, British India.

Died: 5 January 2013 in Islamabad, Pakistan at the age of 74.

questions.title: Against whom did Syed Ahmad Shaheed lead his Jihad movement?
questions.explanation:
Syed Ahmad Shaheed (1786-1831) was a Muslim leader and reformer who founded the Jihad movement in India.

He led a rebellion against the Sikh Empire in the early 19th century.

The Jihad movement, also known as the Tariqah-i-Muhammadiyah.

It aimed to reform Islamic practices, promote religious purification, and resist British colonial rule.

questions.explanation:

سندھ میں دریائے سندھ کی وجہ سے زرخیز زمین دستیاب ہے، جو ایگریکلچر کے لیے موزوں ہے۔

questions.title: Name the country that used Israeli-made Harop Drones to cause panic among Pakistani people in May 2025?
